volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Philoctetes (Section 1290 - Section 1407)

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ εἰπών , εἰ λέγεις ἐτήτυμα .
sections.translation: O welcome words — if your words are true !

sections.label: Section 1291
sections.original: τοὔργον παρέσται φανερόν · ἀλλὰ δεξιὰν
sections.translation: The deed will soon make it plain . Come , stretch out your right hand and be master of your bow ! As he hands the bow and arrows to Philoctetes , Odysseus suddenly appears .

sections.label: Section 1293
sections.original: ἐγὼ δʼ ἀπαυδῶ γʼ , ᾧ θεοὶ ξυνίστορες ,
sections.translation: But I forbid it , as the gods are my witnesses , in the name of the Atreids and the entire army !

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: τέκνον , τίνος φώνημα , μῶν Ὀδυσσέως ,
sections.translation: Son , whose voice was that ? Do I hear Odysseus ?

sections.label: Section 1296a
sections.original: σάφʼ ἴσθι · καὶ πέλας γʼ ὁρᾷς ,
sections.translation: Be sure of it , and you see him at your side , who will carry you to the plains of Troy by force , whether or not the son of Achilles is willing .

sections.label: Section 1299
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔ τι χαίρων , ἢν τόδʼ ὀρθωθῇ βέλος .
sections.translation: But it will bring you no joy , if this arrow fly straight . Odysseus flees from the stage .

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: ἆ , μηδαμῶς , μή , πρὸς θεῶν , μεθῇς βέλος .
sections.translation: Wait — by the gods , no ! Do not let it fly !

sections.label: Section 1301
sections.original: μέθες με , πρὸς θεῶν , χεῖρα , φίλτατον τέκνον .
sections.translation: Let go of me , in the name of the gods , dear boy !

sections.label: Section 1302
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν μεθείην .
sections.translation: I will not .

sections.label: Section 1302a
sections.original: φεῦ · τί μʼ ἄνδρα πολέμιον
sections.translation: Alas ! Why did you take from me the chance to kill my hated enemy with my bow ?

sections.label: Section 1304
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔτʼ ἐμοὶ τοῦτʼ ἐστὶν οὔτε σοὶ καλόν .
sections.translation: It would have been honorable neither for me , nor for you .

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὖν τοσοῦτόν γʼ ἴσθι , τοὺς πρώτους στρατοῦ ,
sections.translation: Well , you may be sure of one thing , at least : the army ’ s chiefs , the lying heralds of the Greeks , though bold with words , are cowards in the fight .

sections.label: Section 1308
sections.original: εἶεν · τὰ μὲν δὴ τόξʼ ἔχεις , κοὐκ ἔσθʼ ὅτου
sections.translation: Good ; the bow is yours , and you have no cause for anger or complaint against me .

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: ξύμφημι · τὴν φύσιν δʼ ἔδειξας , ὦ τέκνον ,
sections.translation: Agreed . You have revealed the true stock , my son , from which you spring . You are no child of Sisyphus , but of Achilles , whose fame was the fairest when he was among the living , as it is now with the dead .

sections.label: Section 1314
sections.original: ἥσθην πατέρα τὸν ἀμὸν εὐλογοῦντά σε
sections.translation: I delight at your praise of my father ,

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: αὐτόν τʼ ἔμʼ · ὧν δέ σου τυχεῖν ἐφίεμαι ,
sections.translation: and of myself . But hear what I desire to gain from you . It is true that men are compelled to bear the fortunes given by the gods ; but when they cling to self-inflicted miseries , as you do ,

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: δίκαιόν ἐστιν οὔτʼ ἐποικτίρειν τινά .
sections.translation: no one can justly excuse or pity them . You have become savage : you welcome no counsellor , and if someone admonishes you , even if he speaks in all good will , you detest him and consider him an enemy who wishes you ill . All the same I will speak to you , calling Zeus who guards oaths to witness .

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτʼ ἐπίστω καὶ γράφου φρενῶν ἔσω .
sections.translation: And you remember these words and write them in your heart : you suffer this plague ’ s affliction in accordance with god-sent fate , because you came near to Chryse ’ s guardian , the serpent who secretly watches over her home and guards her roofless sanctuary . Know also that you will never gain relief from this grave sickness ,

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: νόσου βαρείας , ἕως ἂν αὑτὸς ἥλιος
sections.translation: as long as the sun still rises in the east and sets in the west , until of your own free will you come to the plains of Troy , find there the sons of Asclepius , our comrades , be relieved of this infection , and , with this bow ’ s

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: ξὺν τοῖσδε τόξοις ξύν τʼ ἐμοὶ πέρσας φανῇς .
sections.translation: aid and mine , be hailed as the sacker of Troy ’ s towers . How I know these things are so ordained , I will tell you . We have a Trojan prisoner , Helenus , foremost among seers , who says plainly that all this must come to pass , and further ,

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: ὡς ἔστʼ ἀνάγκη τοῦ παρεστῶτος θέρους
sections.translation: that this very summer must see the complete capture of Troy . Otherwise he willingly gives himself over for execution , if these prophecies of his prove false . Therefore , now that you understand everything , give way graciously . It is a glorious addition to your gain to be singled out

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: κριθέντʼ ἄριστον τοῦτο μὲν παιωνίας
sections.translation: as best of the Greeks — first , for coming into healing hands , and then for taking Troy rich in tears , and so winning a matchless renown .

sections.label: Section 1348
sections.original: ὦ στυγνὸς αἰών , τί με , τί δῆτʼ ἔχεις ἄνω
sections.translation: Hateful life , why , why do you keep me in the light of day , instead of letting me go to Hades ’ domain ?

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί δράσω ; πῶς ἀπιστήσω λόγοις
sections.translation: Ah , me , what shall I do ? How can I ignore this man ’ s words , when he has advised me with good will ? But shall I yield , then ? How , after doing that , shall I , ill-fated , come into men ’ s sight ? Whom will I be able to talk to ? You orbs that have watched my every suffering ,

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: ταῦτʼ ἐξανασχήσεσθε , τοῖσιν Ἀτρέως
sections.translation: how could you endure to see me consorting with the sons of Atreus , who caused my ruin , or with the accursed son of Laertes ? It is not my resentment for what has already been done that stings me , rather it is the many troubles which I seem to foresee I must suffer at the hands of

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: δοκῶ προλεύσσειν · οἷς γὰρ ἡ γνώμη κακῶν
sections.translation: these men in the future . For when the mind of men has once mothered wrongdoing , it trains those men to be wrongdoers in all else thereafter . And in you , too , I wonder at this . You should never yourself revisit Troy , and should prevent me from going there , seeing that those men have done you outrage

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: πατρὸς γέρας συλῶντες , εἶτα τοῖσδε σὺ
sections.translation: by stripping you of your father ’ s arms when , in the suit for the weapons , they judged unhappy Ajax inferior to Odysseus . After that , will you go to fight at their side , and compel me to do the same ? No , do not do it , son , but , as you swore to me , escort me home . You yourself remain in Scyros , and leave those evil men to their evil doom .

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: χοὔτω διπλῆν μὲν ἐξ ἐμοῦ κτήσει χάριν ,
sections.translation: So shall you win double thanks from me , as from your father , and you will not appear through your service to bad men to be like them in your nature .

sections.label: Section 1374
sections.original: θεοῖς τε πιστεύσαντα τοῖς τʼ ἐμοῖς λόγοις
sections.translation: Your recommendation is reasonable , but nevertheless , I wish that you would put your trust in the gods and in my words ,

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: φίλου μετʼ ἀνδρὸς τοῦδε τῆσδʼ ἐκπλεῖν χθονός .
sections.translation: and sail from this land with me , your friend .

sections.label: Section 1376
sections.original: ἦ πρὸς τὰ Τροίας πεδία καὶ τὸν Ἀτρέως
sections.translation: What ! To the plains of Troy and to the abhorred son of Atreus , with this miserable foot ?

sections.label: Section 1378
sections.original: πρὸς τοὺς μὲν οὖν σε τήνδε τʼ ἔμπυον βάσιν
sections.translation: No , rather to those who will free you and your pus-filled limb from pain , and will save you from your sickness .

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: ὦ δεινὸν αἶνον αἰνέσας , τί φής ποτε ;
sections.translation: You giver of frightening advice , what have you said ?

sections.label: Section 1381
sections.original: ἃ σοί τε κἀμοὶ λῷσθʼ ὁρῶ τελούμενα .
sections.translation: I recognize what will be best in the end for you and for me .

sections.label: Section 1382
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτα λέξας οὐ καταισχύνει θεούς ;
sections.translation: Have you no shame before the gods for saying that ?

sections.label: Section 1383
sections.original: πῶς γάρ τις αἰσχύνοιτʼ ἂν ὠφελῶν φίλους ·
sections.translation: Why should a man be ashamed of benefiting his friends ?

sections.label: Section 1384
sections.original: λέγεις δʼ Ἀτρείδαις ὄφελος ἢ ʼπʼ ἐμοὶ τόδε ;
sections.translation: Do you mean a benefit to the Atreids , or for me ?

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: σοί που , φίλος γʼ ὤν , χὠ λόγος τοιόσδε μου .
sections.translation: For you , certainly , since I am your friend and speak in friendship .

sections.label: Section 1386
sections.original: πῶς , ὅς γε τοῖς ἐχθροῖσί μʼ ἐκδοῦναι θέλεις ;
sections.translation: How can that be , when you would give me up to my enemies ?

sections.label: Section 1387
sections.original: ὦ τᾶν , διδάσκου μὴ θρασύνεσθαι κακοῖς .
sections.translation: Please , sir , learn to be less defiant in misfortune .

sections.label: Section 1388
sections.original: ὀλεῖς με , γιγνώσκω σε , τοῖσδε τοῖς λόγοις .
sections.translation: You will ruin me — I know it — with these words .

sections.label: Section 1389
sections.original: οὔκουν ἔγωγε · φημὶ δʼ οὔ σε μανθάνειν .
sections.translation: Not I . But you , I say , will not understand .

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: ἐγὼ οὐκ Ἀτρείδας ἐκβαλόντας οἶδά με ;
sections.translation: Do I not know already that the Atreids cast me away ?

sections.label: Section 1391
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐκβαλόντες εἰ πάλιν σώσουσʼ ὅρα .
sections.translation: They cast you out , yes , but look if they will not in turn restore you .

sections.label: Section 1392
sections.original: οὐδέποθʼ ἑκόντα γʼ ὥστε τὴν Τροίαν ἰδεῖν .
sections.translation: Never — if I must first consent to see Troy .

sections.label: Section 1393
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἂν ἡμεῖς δρῷμεν , εἰ σέ γʼ ἐν λόγοις
sections.translation: What can I do , then if my pleading fails to persuade you of anything that I recommend ?

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: ὡς ῥᾷστʼ ἐμοὶ μὲν τῶν λόγων λῆξαι , σὲ δὲ
sections.translation: The easiest course for me is to stop talking , and for you to live , just as you do now , without deliverance .

sections.label: Section 1397
sections.original: ἔα με πάσχειν ταῦθʼ ἅπερ παθεῖν με δεῖ ·
sections.translation: Let me bear the sufferings that are fated me . But what you promised me with your right hand in mine — to bring me home ,— that promise fulfil for me , son ,

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: καὶ μὴ βράδυνε μηδʼ ἐπιμνησθῇς ἔτι
sections.translation: and do not delay , or remind me further of Troy . I have had my fill of grief and lamentations .

sections.label: Section 1402
sections.original: εἰ δοκεῖ , στείχωμεν .
sections.translation: If it is your will , let us go .

sections.label: Section 1402a
sections.original: ὦ γενναῖον εἰρηκὼς ἔπος .
sections.translation: O noble words !

sections.label: Section 1403
sections.original: ἀντέρειδε νῦν βάσιν σήν .
sections.translation: Now lean your steps firmly upon mine .

sections.label: Section 1403a
sections.original: εἰς ὅσον γʼ ἐγὼ σθένω .
sections.translation: As far as my strength allows .

sections.label: Section 1404
sections.original: αἰτίαν δὲ πῶς Ἀχαιῶν φεύξομαι ;
sections.translation: But how shall I escape blame from the Achaeans ?

sections.label: Section 1404a
sections.original: μὴ φροντίσῃς .
sections.translation: Disregard it .

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: τί γάρ , ἐὰν πορθῶσι χώραν τὴν ἐμήν ;
sections.translation: What if they ravage my country ?

sections.label: Section 1405a
sections.original: ἐγὼ παρὼν
sections.translation: I will be there —

sections.label: Section 1406
sections.original: τίνα προσωφέλησιν ἔρξεις ;
sections.translation: And what aid will you render ?

sections.label: Section 1406a
sections.original: βέλεσι τοῖς Ἡρακλέους
sections.translation: — And with the shafts of Heracles —

sections.label: Section 1407
sections.original: πῶς λέγεις ;
sections.translation: What ?
